page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-6xCPU-16GB is a KVM-based virtual server belonging to the CLOUDNATIVE family. It features 6 vCPUs mapped to 6 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared, and the architecture is x86_64. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.67 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a split performance profile. The shared CPU allocation results in poor single-core CPU performance and weak multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng testing. However, memory bandwidth remains competitive, scoring in the average range for both cached small-block reads and uncached large-block read/write operations. Geekbench and Passmark CPU tests also place this server in the average tier, indicating that while raw synthetic CPU stress tests are low, real-world instruction execution, such as software compilation and image processing, performs adequately.

The primary tradeoff of this server is its shared CPU model, which limits sustained high-compute workloads and results in weak static web serving throughput. However, the 16.0 GB memory capacity and average memory bandwidth provide a cost-effective resource pool for memory-bound but compute-relaxed applications. It is best suited for development environments, lightweight database operations, text processing, and basic background worker tasks that do not require dedicated CPU threads.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-6xCPU-16GB is a KVM-based virtual server configured with 6 vCPUs on a shared AMD EPYC 7542 processor and 16.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Benchmark results indicate poor single-core and weak multi-core CPU performance under heavy stress-ng testing, a direct consequence of its shared CPU allocation. Conversely, memory bandwidth and Geekbench scores align with the average tier, showing stable performance for memory operations and general instruction execution. Given the shared CPU model and lack of bundled storage, this server offers a cost-effective option for workloads that prioritize memory capacity over sustained raw compute power. It is well-suited for development environments, text processing, and lightweight database tasks.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-6xCPU-16GB is a KVM virtual server with 6 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, offering average memory performance for budget-conscious development and light database workloads.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-6xCPU-16GB features 6 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, suitable for memory-focused, low-compute development workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly shared-CPU virtual server offering balanced memory capacity and average bandwidth for non-compute-intensive development workloads.
bullet_points: 6 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs on x86_64 architecture
16.0 GB of system memory with average read/write bandwidth
No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware included
KVM hypervisor virtualization with shared CPU allocation
Best suited for development environments and light database tasks
